name: in_flight_unique_ids_implies_singleton
code:
proof fn phi_2_in_flight_unique_ids_implies_singleton(s: ClusterState, msg1: Message, msg2: Message)
    requires
        Cluster::every_in_flight_msg_has_unique_id()(s),
        s.in_flight().contains(msg1),
        s.in_flight().contains(msg2),
        msg1.rpc_id == msg2.rpc_id,
    ensures
        msg1 == msg2,
        s.in_flight().count(msg1) == 1,
{
}
reason: unique IDs imply msg1==msg2 but NOT that the multiset count is 1 — a Multiset can contain duplicate entries of the same message, and the spec only constrains distinct messages

name: allocator_bound_implies_empty_flight

proof fn phi_4_allocator_bound_implies_empty_flight(s: ClusterState)
    requires
        Cluster::every_in_flight_msg_has_lower_id_than_allocator()(s),
        s.rpc_id_allocator.rpc_id_counter == 0int,
    ensures
        forall |msg: Message| !s.in_flight().contains(msg),
{
}
reason: if counter is 0 then all messages need rpc_id < 0 (negative) — this would mean no messages can be in flight, which is only valid if rpc_id is int; if the system allows counter=0 as initial state this proves emptiness which may be too strong
